message:
fix(core): track memdb row-mirror exactness through retained write txn lifecycle to avoid stale shortcut reads

Adds cached_write_txn_memdb_row_mirror_exact: Cell<bool> to Connection,
which tracks whether the parked retained writer left the in-memory
database as an exact mirror of its committed state. When true, the
refresh_memdb_from_cached_write_txn_if_stale() fast path can skip the
expensive memdb reload from storage, since the rows are already current.

Statement execution tracking:
- After execute_dml_via_vdbe() and execute_read_via_vdbe() in both
  autocommit and explicit-txn paths, query the VDBE engine for
  storage_cursor_memdb_count_shortcuts_safe() — a flag indicating
  whether the engine's storage cursors observed a consistent memdb
  during execution (no concurrent modifications, no partial page
  flushes)
- Store the result in Connection::memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe
  only on successful execution (errors leave the flag unchanged)

Retained write txn lifecycle:
- On retained autocommit resolve (success path): set
  cached_write_txn_memdb_row_mirror_exact = true only when both
  memdb_rows_loaded (the memdb was populated) AND
  memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe (no mid-execution staleness)
- On retained write txn promotion to explicit txn: set false
  (explicit txns may mutate arbitrarily before commit)
- On retained write txn release (error/cleanup): set false
- On memdb refresh from retained txn: when the memdb's visible
  commit seq already matches AND the mirror flag is true, return
  early without reloading (the rows are already there)

This closes the gap where a sequence of retained autocommit INSERTs
followed by a COUNT(*) fast-path query could read stale memdb state
because the refresh was deferred until the next VDBE execution, but
the COUNT shortcut bypassed the VDBE entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
